unit.note: Server vitest 163 files / 1911 tests green (F0 gate, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local); server tsc --noEmit clean + build green. D08 is server-only (server/src/core/project-manager.ts, new server/src/core/project-registry-io.ts, new server/src/core/project-manager-corrupt-load.test.ts, server/src/index.ts) so the client suite (1971 tests, unchanged since D07 - no D08 client files) was not re-run. New F07 coverage: project-manager-corrupt-load.test.ts (15 tests: ProjectManager.load over empty/whitespace/truncated/non-array/well-formed + atomic-vs-plain persist; project-registry-io parse/load/quarantine/atomic-write units).

integration.note: Real-filesystem confidence-calibration probe (server/dist shipped module, Windows 11): (1) fs.promises.rename OVERWRITES an existing projects.json dest (overwrote=true, tmpGone=true, no error) - empirically refutes the external code-review claim that Windows fs.rename cannot replace an existing file; Node maps it to MoveFileExW|MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING, same proven path as sdk-sessions-store atomicWriteFile. (2) round-trip corrupt->quarantine->re-persist: truncated file loads empty, is renamed aside to .corrupt-<ts>-<uuid>, live file moved away, a subsequent atomic persist writes valid JSON, no tmp left behind. (3) empty file -> empty map, no quarantine.

e2e.note: No Playwright run. D08 is a server-only boot-path / persistence hardening of the projects.json registry (ProjectManager.load + persist) with NO UI-consumed contract change: no route shape, DOM, prop, Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface changed. The recovery is observable only at server boot (a corrupt file no longer FATAL-exits). A browser smoke adds no signal the deterministic server Vitest + real-fs round-trip probe already provide. F0.5 surface=none.

surface_verification.justification: Server-only ProjectManager registry boot-load / persist hardening (F07). No UI-consumed contract changed: no /api route shape, no DOM/prop/render, no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface. The behavior is observable only at server startup - a truncated/empty projects.json no longer makes Hono exit FATAL. AC2 correctness is proven by RED-first server Vitest (project-manager-corrupt-load.test.ts: on pre-fix tree ProjectManager.load throws SyntaxError at project-manager.ts:96 for empty/whitespace/truncated/non-array content, and persist is non-atomic; all GREEN after) plus a real-filesystem round-trip probe against the shipped dist module. A browser run would add no signal the deterministic unit + integration tests lack, so F0.5 surface=none (no fabricated browser run).

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - ProjectManager.load() tolerates a corrupt projects.json: empty/whitespace loads an empty registry (no quarantine); truncated/unparseable/non-array content is quarantined aside to projects.json.corrupt-<ts>-<uuid> (best-effort, never re-throws) and boot continues with an empty registry instead of exiting FATAL; persist() writes atomically via tmp+rename.
behaviors.evidence: server/src/core/project-manager-corrupt-load.test.ts - ProjectManager.load over '' / whitespace / truncated JSON (renames aside) / non-array (quarantines) / well-formed (populates); persist atomic tmp+rename when a rename dep is present (asserts NO direct write to the live path) + plain in-place fallback when absent; project-registry-io units for parse/load/quarantine (incl. never-throws-on-fs-failure + copy-aside fallback) and atomic-write. Real-fs round-trip probe (integration block) confirms the full producer/consumer chain on Windows.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression proof is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after
behaviors.evidence: RED captured on pre-fix tree (HEAD project-manager.ts restored, new io module present): 5 ProjectManager-level cases fail - empty/whitespace/truncated/non-array all throw SyntaxError 'Unexpected end of JSON input' / 'Unterminated string' / 'projects is not iterable' at project-manager.ts:96-97, and the atomic-persist case fails (0 tmp writes, no rename). GREEN after the fix (49 tests across the two project-manager test files).

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server suite + build stay green (no invariant/ADR regressed)
behaviors.evidence: F0: server vitest 163 files / 1911/1911 (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local); server tsc --noEmit clean + build green. project-delete-unassigned.integration.test.ts + all sdk-sessions/store tests green. CLAUDE.md read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact (writes only projects.json + its quarantine/tmp side-files; never ~/.claude/projects/** or shipwright_run_config.json). Client untouched (server-only iterate).

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted
behaviors.evidence: All three baselined touch-files stay at or below their ceilings (anti-ratchet hook exit 0): project-manager.ts 420==420 (delegates to the new module), index.ts 892<899 (verbose inline deps type replaced with the exported ProjectManagerDeps to make room for the rename dep wiring). New non-baselined files below the 300 limit: project-registry-io.ts 164, project-manager-corrupt-load.test.ts 254. project-manager.test.ts unchanged at 480. shipwright_bloat_baseline.json not modified (no ratchet).
